The Farm and Conservation Grant (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Farm and Conservation Grant Regulations 1991, as amended (“the principal Regulations”), which implement provisions in Title IV of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2328/91 (OJ No. L218, 6.8.91, p.1) on improving the efficiency of agricultural structures.

items.title: The A1 Trunk Road (Haringey) Red Route Traffic Order 1993 Variation Order 1994
The A1 Trunk Road (Haringey) (Bus Lanes) Red Route Traffic Order 1993 Variation Order 1994
The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) Red Route Traffic Order 1993 Variation Order 1994
The A1 Trunk Road (Islington) (Bus Lanes) Red Route Traffic Order 1993 Variation Order 1994
The Capital Gains Tax (Annual Exempt Amount) Order 1994
The Retirement Benefits Schemes (Indexation of Earnings Cap) Order 1994
items.description: Section 590C(1) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the Act”) provides that in arriving at an employee’s final remuneration for the purposes of section 590(3)(a) or (d) of the Act (conditions for approval of retirement benefit schemes), any excess over the permitted maximum figure (“the earnings cap”) for the year of assessment in which the employee’s participation in the scheme ceases shall be disregarded.  Section 590C(3) fixed the earnings cap for the years of assessment 1988-89 and 1989-90 at £60,000.

items.title: The Income Tax (Cash Equivalents of Car Fuel Benefits) Order 1994
The Inheritance Tax (Indexation) Order 1994
The Income Tax (Indexation) Order 1994
items.description: Sections 1(6) and 257C(3) of the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 (“the Act”) operate to provide that the Treasury shall by order made by statutory instrument before 6th April 1995 specify the amounts which by virtue of those sections shall, unless Parliament otherwise determines, be treated as specified for the year 1995-96. These are the lower rate and basic rate limits (section 1 of the Act) and the personal and married couple’s allowances (sections 257 and 257A of the Act respectively).

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Buildings and Land) Order 1994
The Value Added Tax (Transport) Order 1994
The Value Added Tax (General) (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force as stipulated in regulation 1, further amend the Value Added Tax (General) Regulations 1985 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Amendment (No. 2) Order 1994
items.description: This Order further amends the Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Order 1983 (“the principal Order”) which specifies descriptions and classes of prescription only medicines subject to section 58(2) of the Medicines Act 1968, that is to say, medicinal products which (subject to exemptions) may be sold or supplied by retail only in accordance with a prescription given by an appropriate practitioner and which may be administered only by or in accordance with the directions of such a practitioner.

items.title: The Medical Devices Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations (“the Regulations”) implement Council Directive 93/42/EEC (“the Directive”) concerning medical devices.

items.title: The Accounts and Audit (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: The Accounts and Audit Regulations 1983 make provision in respect of the accounts and the audit of certain bodies whose accounts are required to be audited in accordance with Part III of the Local Government Finance Act 1982.

items.title: The Electricity Supply (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 30 of the Electricity Supply Regulations 1988 (“the 1988 Regulations”) to require every supplier of low voltage electricity to consumers to supply, unless otherwise agreed between the supplier and the consumer, at 230 volts (reduced from 240 volts) between the phase and neutral conductors at the supply terminals, or at a voltage not exceeding ten per cent above (increased from six per cent) or six percent below that voltage. These amendments implement the first transitional stage for European harmonisation of nominal voltages and voltage tolerance ranges proposed in CENELEC (European Committee for Electrotechnical Standardisation) harmonisation document HD 472 S1: 1988 entitled Nominal voltages for low voltage public electricity supply systems and published in the United Kingdom by the British Standards Institution as BS 7697: 1993. The standard may be obtained from the British Standards Institution at the address below at a cost of £12.25 for members and £24.50 for non-members.

items.title: The Firearms (Amendment) Rules 1994 (revoked)
The Charities Act 1992 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1994
The Charitable Institutions (Fund-Raising) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations, which extend to England and Wales only, are made in pursuance of sections 59 and 64 of the Charities Act 1992 (“the 1992 Act”) for purposes connected with certain other provisions of Part II of that Act (control of fund-raising for charitable institutions).

items.title: The Local Government (Compensation for Redundancy) Regulations 1994
items.description: The purpose of these Regulations is to allow a lump sum payment to be made to certain employees who cease to hold local government employment by reason of redundancy or in the interests of the efficiency of their employer. The Regulations are divided into five Parts. Part I sets out various definitions.

items.title: The Local Government Superannuation (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations make a number of amendments to the Local Government Superannuation Regulations 1986 (“the principal Regulations”). All the regulations, except regulations 1, 4 and 9, are made retrospective in effect by virtue of section 12 of the Superannuation Act 1972.

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Pension Business) (Transitional Provisions) (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: Schedule 19AB to the Income and Corporation Taxes Act 1988 provides that insurance companies carrying on pension business shall be entitled to provisional repayments of tax on pension business investment income. However, during a transitional period, the Commissioners of Inland Revenue may make provision, by regulations, for the amount of a provisional repayment to be reduced by a prescribed percentage for any accounting period ending during that transitional period. The regulations may prescribe different percentages for accounting periods ending after different dates.

items.title: The Deregulation and Contracting Out Act 1994 (Commencement No.1) Order 1994
The National Health Service (Service Committees and Tribunal) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Service Committees and Tribunal) (Scotland) Regulations 1992 (“the principal Regulations”). Regulation 2 amends regulation 3 of the principal Regulations to make clear that the Health Board to which the complaint should be addressed is the one on whose list the doctor (including when a deputy acted for him), dentist, optician or pharmacist was at the time of the matter which is the subject of the complaint. Regulations 2 and 3 amend regulations 3 and 8 respectively of the principal Regulations to extend the time limit during which a complaint against a dentist may be made. Regulations 4, 5 and 6 amend respectively regulations 11 and 12 of and Schedule 1 to the principal Regulations to provide for an appeal to the Secretary of State by a complainer in circumstances where the Health Board determine that no further action should be taken on a complaint. Regulation 6 also amends paragraph 20 of Schedule 1 to the principal Regulations to permit as of right any member of the Council on Tribunals or the Scottish Committee thereof to be present during the Service Committee’s deliberations following a hearing.

items.title: The Police (Special Constables) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police (Special Constables) (Scotland) Regulations 1966 so as to remove the minimum height requirement in relation to appointment to the office of special constable.

items.title: The Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1994
The Excise Duty (Amendment of the Isle of Man Act 1979) Order 1994
The Education (Fees and Awards) Regulations 1994
The Education (Mandatory Awards) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1994
The Education (Mandatory Awards) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st September 1995, supersede the Education (Mandatory Awards) (No. 2) Regulations 1993.

items.title: The Education (Student Loans) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 1st August 1995, supersede the Education (Student Loans) (No 2) Regulations 1993 which are revoked (regulation 2).

items.title: THE COURT OF PROTECTION RULES 1994
items.description: These Rules replace the Court of Protection Rules 1984. They regulate the procedure in respect of applications under the Mental Health Act 1983 and provide for the division of functions between the Court of Protection and the Public Trustee.

items.title: The Court of Protection (Enduring Powers of Attorney) Rules 1994
items.description: These Rules replace the Court of Protection (Enduring Powers of Attorney) Rules 1986. They are supplemental to the Court of Protection Rules 1994 (S.I. 1994/.) They regulate the procedure in respect of applications under the Enduring Powers of Attorney Act 1985 and provide for the division of functions between the Court of Protection and the Public Trustee. Schedule 2 restates the fees payable in connection with an application for registration of an enduring power of attorney and an application for a search of the register.

items.title: The Merchant Shipping (Liability of Shipowners and Others) (Rate of Interest) Order 1994
The Veterinary Surgeons and Veterinary Practitioners (Registration) (Amendment) Regulations Order of Council 1994
items.description: The Regulations approved by this Order further amend the Veterinary Surgeons and Veterinary Practitioners Registration Regulations 1967 by increasing, with effect from 31st March 1994, the fees payable for the Registration and annual retention of names on the registers of the Royal College of Veterinary Surgeons. The existing fees, which came into effect on 31st March 1993, are being increased by 3.1 per cent. The Order also revokes a 1975 Order containing spent Regulations.

items.title: The Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Amendment (No. 3) Order 1994
items.description: This Order further amends the Medicines (Products Other Than Veterinary Drugs) (Prescription Only) Order 1983 (“the principal Order”) which specifies descriptions and classes of prescription only medicines subject to section 58(2) of the Medicines Act 1968, that is to say, medicinal products which (subject to exemptions) may be sold or supplied by retail only in accordance with a prescription given by an appropriate practitioner and which may be administered only by or in accordance with the directions of such a practitioner.

items.title: The Construction Products (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the Construction Products Regualations 1991 to give effect to Council Directive 93/68/EEC. This amends the CE marking provisions under a number of Directives including the Directive relating to construction products.

items.title: The Water Byelaws (Loch an Sgoltaire) Extension Order 1994
The Local Government Changes for England (Non-Domestic Rating) (Contributions) Regulations 1994
items.description: Part II of the Local Government Act 1992 makes provision for local government changes in England.  Where recommendations for change are made by the Local Government Commission, the Secretary of State may make an order giving effect to those recommendations (“a reorganisation order”).

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Joint Financing) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Civil Aviation (Joint Financing) Regulations 1988, as amended.

items.title: The Cambridgeshire County Council (River Nene B1040 Dog–In–A–Doublet Bridge) Scheme 1994 Confirmation Instrument 1994
The County Court (Amendment) Rules 1994
The Income-related Benefits Schemes (Miscellaneous Amendments) (No. 6) Regulations 1994
The Doncaster Area Drainage Act 1929 (Amendment) Order 1994
items.description: The Doncaster Area Drainage Acts 1929 and 1933 impose obligations on mine owners to construct and maintain, and provide funds for the construction and maintenance of, drainage works. The Acts provide for the transfer of the works and funds to the National Rivers Authority (which exercises the functions of the former Ouse and Trent Catchment Boards) in the event of a mine permanently ceasing to be worked.

items.title: The Coal Industry Act 1994 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1994
items.description: This Order brings into force certain of the repeals specified in Part III of Schedule 11 to the Coal Industry Act 1994 not previously brought into force.

items.title: The Coal Mining Subsidence (Land Drainage) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the manner in which questions with respect to subsidence damage affecting land drainage, arising between persons responsible for such damage and the appropriate drainage authorities under section 36 of the Coal Mining Subsidence Act 1991 (as amended by the Coal Industry Act 1994), are to be determined, and prescribe the procedure to be followed after measures in relation to such damage have been agreed, determined or varied. The Regulations largely reproduce the effect of S. I. 1958/1486, with appropriate amendments to take account of the 1991 and 1994 Acts.

items.title: The Aire and Calder Navigation Act 1992 (Amendment) Order 1994
items.description: The Aire and Calder Navigation Act 1992 confers certain rights with associated liabilities on the British Coal Corporation in respect of the St. Aidan’s Remainder Site. This Order transfers these rights and liabilities to British Coal’s successor as licensed operator under the Coal Industry Act 1994 and any subsequent successors.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Copyright, Designs and Patents) (Amendment) 1994
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Copyright, Designs and Patents) 1990 by deleting reference to section 58C of the Trade Marks Act 1938, now repealed, and by making provision for the procedure for an application under sections 16 and 19 of the Trade Marks Act 1994.

items.title: The Environmentally Sensitive Areas (Scotland) Orders Amendment Order 1994
The Local Government (Compensation for Redundancy) (Scotland) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations allow a lump sum payment to be made to certain employees falling within the definition of eligible employees who cease to hold local government employment by reason of redundancy or in the interests of the efficiency of their employing authority.

items.title: The Insurance Brokers Registration Council (Registration and Enrolment) (Amendment) Rules Approval Order 1994
The Royal Hospital of St. Bartholomew, the Royal London Hospital and London Chest Hospital National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order establishes the Royal Hospital of St. Bartholomew, the Royal London Hospital and London Chest Hospital National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Coal Industry (Protected Persons) Pensions Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations are concerned with the industry-wide pension scheme established in accordance with the Industry-Wide Coal Staff Superannuation Scheme Regulations 1994 and the industry-wide pension scheme established in accordance with the Industry-Wide Mineworkers' Pension Scheme Regulations 1994. Both of the regulations were made under the Coal Industry Act 1994. Such an industry-wide scheme is defined in the present Regulations as a “New Scheme”.

items.title: The Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Civil Aviation (Route Charges for Navigation Services) Regulations 1993, as amended.

items.title: The Police and Magistrates' Courts Act 1994 (Commencement No.4 and Transitional Provisions) (Scotland) Order 1994
items.description: This Order brings into force for specified purposes various provisions of Part II (which concern the arrangements for policing in Scotland) of the Police and Magistrates' Courts Act 1994.

items.title: The Energy Information (Refrigerators and Freezers) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement Commission Directive 94/2/EC and Council Directive 92/75/EEC (on giving information about energy consumption of household appliances) in relation to the mains electric household refrigerators, freezers and their combinations described in regulation 3.

items.title: The Education (London Residuary Body) (Property Transfer) (Amendment) Order 1994
The District Probate Registries (Amendment No. 2) Order 1994
The Newham Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order establishes the Newham Healthcare National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Electromagnetic Compatibility (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 93/68/EEC (the “CE Marking Directive”) (regulation 4) as it amends Council Directive 89/336/EEC (the EMC Directive) by amending the Electromagnetic Compatibility Regulations 1992 (S. I. No. 2372; “the EMC Regulations”). Provision is made for the CE mark to continue to be applied until 31st December 1996 pursuant to the transitional period in Article 14.2 of the CE Marking Directive (regulation 5).

items.title: The Coal Industry Act 1994 (Consequential Modifications of Local Acts) Order 1994
items.description: This Order makes modifications to local Acts in consequence of the enactment of the Coal Industry Act 1994. Articles 2 and 3 amend the local Acts in the manner set out in Schedules 1 and 2, the modifications including the substitution of references to the Coal Authority or licensed operators under the Coal Industry Act 1994 for references to the British Coal Corporation.

items.title: The Meat Products (Hygiene) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 92/5/EEC (OJ No. L57, 2.3.92, p.1)(“the Directive”) amending and updating Directive 77/99/EEC (OJ No. L26, 31.1.77, p.85) on health problems affecting intra-Community trade in meat products and amending Directive 64/433/EEC (OJ No. 121, 29.7.64, (OJ/SE 1963-64 p.185)), as amended by Article 14(3) of Council Directive 92/45/EEC (OJ No. L268, 14.9.92, p.35) read with Commission Decision 94/383/EC (OJ No. L174, 8.7.94, p.33). The Regulations relate to States (other than Iceland) in the European Economic Area. This is by reason of the EEA Agreement (at OJ L1, 3.1.94) and Decision No. 7/94 of the EEA Joint Committee (see Annex 2 at OJ L160/13, 28.6.94).

items.title: The Boiler (Efficiency) (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations extend to the European Economic Area the Boiler (Efficiency) Regulations 1993 (“the 1993 Regulations”) which concern efficiency requirements for new water boilers fired with liquid or gaseous fuels. They also complete the implementation of the amendments made to Council Directive 92/42/EEC by Council Directive 93/68/EEC by amending the provisions in regulations 8(1), and paragraphs 4(4) and 9(5) of Schedule 5 of the 1993 Regulations.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Livingston Development Corporation) Order 1994
The Set-Aside Access (Scotland) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement in part Council Regulation (EEC) No.2078/92 (OJ No.L215, 30.7.92, p.85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and maintenance of the countryside.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Proceedings in the Sheriff Court under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987) (Amendment) 1994
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Proceedings in the Sheriff Court under the Debtors (Scotland) Act 1987) 1988 to make provision for recovery of insurance premium tax by summary warrant procedure.

items.title: The Tavistock and Portman National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order establishes the Tavistock and Portman National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Treatment of Spruce Bark (Amendment) Order 1994
The Plant Health (Forestry) (Great Britain) (Amendment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order amends the Plant Health (Forestry) (Great Britain) Order 1993 and implements the following Community legislation—

items.title: The Vehicle Licences (Duration of First Licences and Rate of Duty) (Amendment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order amends the Vehicle Licences (Duration of First Licences and Rate of Duty) Order 1986. That Order is extended to Northern Ireland in place of the Vehicle Licences (Duration of First Licences and Rate of Duty) (Northern Ireland) Order 1986, which is revoked. There is an increase of £1 in each case in the rates at which additional vehicle excise duty is payable where the first licence for a vehicle (for which the annual rate of duty does not exceed £300) is taken out for a period of 6 or 12 months plus a period beginning on the 10th, 17th or 24th day of the first month. This is the first increase in these rates since October 1986. References to repealed legislation are updated.

items.title: The Highlands and Islands Agricultural Programme Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations lay down measures necessary to implement Council Regulation (EEC) No.4256/88 laying down provisions for implementing Council Regulation (EEC) No.2052/88 as amended by Council Regulation (EEC) No.2085/93 as regards the European Agricultural Guidance and Guarantee Fund (EAGGF) Guidance Section and enable financial assistance to be received from that Section under an approved measure contained in the Single Programming Document (SPD) for the Highlands and Islands Objective 1 area approved by the EC Commission on 29th July 1994.

items.title: The Opencast Coal (Compulsory Rights, Drainage and Rights of Way) (Forms) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the forms of certain orders under the Opencast Coal Act 1958 and the forms of the notices and advertisements to be given in connection with applications for, and the making and confirming of such orders. The orders in question are orders conferring compulsorily temporary rights of occupation, and of use of land for drainage and water supply purposes, on opencast coal operators to facilitate their operations and orders temporarily suspending non-vehicular rights of way for the same purpose.

items.title: The Simple Pressure Vessels (Safety) (Amendment) Regulations 1994 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Simple Pressure Vessels (Safety) Regulations 1991 (S. I.1991/2749) (“the principal Regulations”). They implement Article 2 of Council Directive 93/68/EEC (“the CE Marking Directive”), which amends Council Directive 87/404/EEC on the harmonisation of the laws of the member States relating to simple pressure vessels, as amended by Council Directive 90/488/EEC, and make some other changes to the principal Regulations. The amendments come into force on 1st January 1995.

items.title: The Habitat (Broadleaved Woodland) (Wales) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement in part Council Regulation (EEC) No.2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92 p.85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside.

items.title: The Forest Healthcare National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1994
items.description: This Order transfers to the Forest Healthcare National Health Service Trust on 9th February 1994 trust property formerly held by the Redbridge and Waltham Forest Health Authority.  A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained at the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Alteration of Boundaries of the Newark Area and Upper Witham Internal Drainage Districts Order 1993
items.description: This Order confirms a Scheme submitted by the National Rivers Authority for the alteration of the boundaries of the Newark Area Internal Drainage District and the Upper Witham Internal Drainage District.

items.title: The Habitat (Water Fringe) (Wales) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement in part Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p.85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside.

items.title: The Habitat (Coastal Belt) (Wales) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement in part Council Regulation (EEC) No.2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92 p.85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside.

items.title: The Habitat (Species-Rich Grassland) (Wales) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement in part Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2078/92 (OJ No. L215, 30.7.92, p.85) on agricultural production methods compatible with the requirements of the protection of the environment and the maintenance of the countryside.

items.title: The Licensing (Fees) (Amendment) Order 1994
The London Residuary Body (Pits at Stone) Order 1994
items.description: This Order gives effect, with modifications, to a scheme submitted by the London Residuary Body for the transfer of certain rights and liabilities.

items.title: The Education, (London Residuary Body) (Property Transfer) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1994
The Education (University Commissioners) Order 1994
The Local Government Act 1988 (Supervision of Parking) (Exemption) (Scotland) Order 1994
The Rent Officers (Additional Functions) (Scotland) Amendment (No.2) Order 1994
The Public Health (International Trains) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations provide for public health controls in relation to international trains which use the channel tunnel.

items.title: The Local Government Changes for England (Collection Fund Surpluses and Deficits) Regulations 1994
items.description: Part II of the Local Government Act 1992 makes provision for local government changes in England. Where recommendations for change are made by the Local Government Commission, the Secretary of State may make an order giving effect to those recommendations (a “reorganisation order”).

items.title: The Marriage Act 1994 (Commencement No. 1) Order 1994
The Motor Vehicle Tyres (Safety) Regulations 1994
The Church Representation Rules (Amendment) Resolution 1994
items.description: This Resolution of the General Synod of the Church of England, which was passed in accordance with section 7(1) of the Synodical Government Measure 1969, amends the Church Representation Rules contained in Schedule 3 to that Measure. The changes include making provision for dual membership, for changes in the Rules required by the Incumbents (Vacation of Benefices) (Amendment) Measure 1993 and the Charities Act 1993, for modifications of the retired clergy franchise and for modifications to the rules concerning elections and the appeal procedure.

items.title: The Medical Devices (Consequential Amendments—Medicines) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the Medicines Act 1968 (“the Act”) and Orders under it in consequence of the implementation of Council Directive 93/42/EEC concerning medical devices (O.J. No. L169 12.7.93 p.1) by the Medical Devices Regulations 1994 (S.I. No. 3017).

items.title: The Medicines (Committee on Dental and Surgical Materials) (Revocation) Order 1994
items.description: This Order revokes the Medicines (Committee on Dental and Surgical Materials) Order 1975 (which established the Committee on Dental and Surgical Materials) and the Medicines (Committee on Dental and Surgical Materials) Amendment Order 1979 (which amended it). This Order abolishes that Committee, the purposes of which were—

items.title: The Central Rating Lists Regulations 1994
items.description: Under section 53(1) of the Local Government Finance Act 1988, the Secretary of State may, with a view to securing the central rating en bloc of certain hereditaments, by regulations designate a person and prescribe in relation to him one or more descriptions of non-domestic hereditament.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Miscellaneous Provisions) (No. 2) (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: Regulation 2 of the Non-Domestic Rating (Miscellaneous Provisions) (No. 2) Regulations 1989 specifies the percentage rate to be applied in relation to the notional cost of construction where a hereditament is valued by reference to the notional cost of constructing or providing it. Regulation 2 of these Regulations adjusts this rate in respect of hereditaments shown in a non-domestic rating list compiled on or after 1st April 1995 and substitutes a definition of “healthcare hereditament” for the definition of “hospital” for the purpose of valuing such hereditaments.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Railways, Telecommunications and Canals) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations provide in relation to England and Wales that certain property occupied, or, if unoccupied, owned by the British Railways Board, Railtrack PLC, Docklands Light Railway Limited, the Tyne and Wear Passenger Transport Executive, Greater Manchester Metro Limited, South Yorkshire Supertram Limited, British Telecommunications plc, Mercury Communications Limited, BR Telecommunications Limited, Energis Communications Limited, AT& T (UK) Limited or the British Waterways Board which would, apart from these Regulations, be more than one hereditament shall be treated as one hereditament. The Regulations also specify who shall be treated as occupying each hereditament.

items.title: The Local Government Reorganisation (Wales) (Transitional Provisions) Order 1994
items.description: Section 46 of the Local Government (Wales) Act 1994 establishes committees comprising of representatives of the existing Welsh principal councils whose areas will be wholly or partly within the area of any new principal council created by the 1994 Act. These committees are known as transition committees and their function is to consider and advise on matters relevant to ensuring that the new Welsh councils will be able to function effectively from 1st April 1996.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (Wales) (Amendment) (No. 3) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations apply to Wales.

items.title: The Value Added Tax (Means of Transport) Order 1994
The Telecommunications Terminal Equipment (Amendment and Extension) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend and extend the application of the Telecommunications Terminal Equipment Regulations 1992 (S. I. No.2423; “the TTE Regulations”), which implemented Council Directive 91/263/EEC on the approximation of the laws of the Member States concerning telecommunications terminal equipment, including the mutual recognition of their conformity (“the TTE Directive”).

items.title: The Vocational Training for General Medical Practice (European Requirements) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations arise out of Title IV of Council Directive 93/16/EEC of 5 April 1993 to facilitate the free movement of doctors and the mutual recognition of their diplomas, certificates and other evidence of formal qualifications (which is referred to in these Regulations as the “Medical Directive”). The Medical Directive consolidated earlier Directives. Title IV is about vocational training for doctors providing general medical services in the national social security schemes of the member States, and comes fully into effect on 1st January 1995.

items.title: The Beef Special Premium (Amendment) Regulations 1994
The Insurance Companies (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the Insurance Companies Act 1982 (“the 1982 Act”), the Banking Act 1987 and the Insurance Companies (Third Insurance Directives) Regulations 1994 (S. I.1994/1696) (“the Third Insurance Directives Regulations”).

items.title: The Insurance Companies (Amendment No. 2) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the Insurance Companies Regulations 1994 (“the 1994 Regulations”) and the Insurance Companies (Accounts and Statements) Regulations 1983 (“the 1983 Regulations”). They make amendments consequential to the amendments made to the Insurance Companies Act 1982 by the Insurance Companies (Amendment) Regulations 1994 which implement the Agreement on the European Economic Area as regards the application of Council Directive 92/49/EEC (O.J. No. L228, 11.8.92, p.1) and Council Directive 92/96/EEC (O.J. No. L360, 9.12.92, p.1) to Austria, Iceland, Norway and Sweden, and, in the case of Directive 92/96 EEC, to Finland and other unrelated amendments.

items.title: The A69 Trunk Road (Haltwhistle Bypass) Order 1994
The A69 Trunk Road (Haltwhistle Town) (De-Trunking) Order 1994
The Legal Aid in Criminal and Care Proceedings (General) (Amendment) (No. 2) Regulations 1994
The Children (Allocation of Proceedings) (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1994
items.description: This Order amends the Children (Allocation of Proceedings) Order 1991 (“the 1991 Order”) by nominating additional courts as Family Hearing Centres and Care Centres. The new Family Hearing Centres are the county courts at Dudley, Grimsby, Bedford, Dartford, King’s Lynn, Bath and Weymouth. The new Care Centres are the county courts at Canterbury and Swindon. The Care Centre at Llangefni County Court is closed. In addition the Order provides that proceedings arising in the petty sessions areas of Machynlleth, Newtown, and Welshpool which are to be transferred to a Care Centre, shall be transferred to Chester County Court.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (England) (Amendment No. 3) Regulations 1994
items.description: Under Part II of Schedule 8 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, billing authorities are required to pay amounts (called non-domestic rating contributions) to the Secretary of State. Payments in respect of a provisional amount of the contributions are made during the financial year, final calculations and any adjustments of payments being made after the year ends.

items.title: The Construction (Design and Management) Regulations 1994
The Diseases of Poultry Order 1994
items.description: This Order (read with the Animal Health Act 1981) implements Council Directive 92/40/EEC introducing Community measures for the control of avian influenza and Council Directive 92/66/EEC introducing Community measures for the control of Newcastle disease (OJ No L 167, 22.6.92. p.1 and L260, 5.9.92, p.1).

items.title: The Marketing Authorisations for Veterinary Medicinal Products Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 93/40/EEC, which is the latest in a series of amendments to Council Directive 81/851/EEC. They provide for veterinary medicinal products to be placed on the market subject to marketing authorisations rather than the previous system of product licences. These authorisations may be granted either by the Ministers or, in accordance with Council Regulation 2309/93/EEC, by the European Agency for the Evaluation of Medicinal Products (regulation 3).

items.title: The Medicines (Veterinary Drugs) (Renewal Applications for Licences and Animal Test Certificates) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Medicines (Veterinary Drugs) (Renewal Applications for Licences and Animal Test Certificates) Regulations 1993 (S. I.1993/1227). They prescribe the form and manner of and the particulars to be contained in or to accompany renewal applications for licences for veterinary drugs and for animal test certificates. In the case of product licences, these Regulations apply to those licences which are not converted to marketing authorisations by virtue of the Marketing Authorisations for Veterinary Medicinal Products Regulations 1994, S. I. 1994/3142; but do not apply to product licences in respect of which renewal applications are made in consequence of a notice served under section 24(1A) of the Medicines Act 1968 (to which the Medicines (Veterinary Medicinal Products) (Renewal Applications for Product Licences Subject to Review) Regulations 1993, S. I.1993/2399, apply).

items.title: The Medicines for Human Use (Marketing Authorisations Etc.) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement for the United Kingdom, and in so far as they are concerned with medicinal products for human use, the Council Directives mentioned in regulation 1(2) in the definition of “relevant Community provisions”. They also contain provisions supplementing the provisions of Council Regulation (EEC) No. 2309/93. Both the Directives and the Regulation are concerned with the marketing of medicinal products.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1994
items.description: Under Part III of Schedule 12 to the Local Government Finance Act 1992, Scottish levying authorities are required to pay amounts (called non-domestic rating contributions) to the Secretary of State. Payments in respect of a provisional amount of the contribution are made during the financial year, final calculations and payments being made after the year ends. These Regulations amend, as regards financial year 1995-96 and subsequent financial years, the rules for the calculation of payments contained in the Non-Domestic Rating Contributions (Scotland) Regulations 1992.

items.title: The Parental Order Register (Form of Entry) (Scotland) Regulations 1994
The Education (European Community Enlargement) (Scotland) Regulations 1994
The Self-Governing Schools (Suspension of Proposals) (Scotland) Order 1994
The Local Government etc. (Scotland) Act 1994 (Commencement No.2) Order 1994
items.description: This Order provides for the commencement of various provisions in the Local Government etc. (Scotland) Act 1994 on 31st December 1994 (article 2), on 4th January 1995 (article 3) and on 1st April 1995 (article 4 and Schedules 1 and 2). The provisions commenced are principally concerned with rating and valuation matters.

items.title: The Registration of Births, Still-births, Deaths and Marriages (Prescription of Forms) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1994
The Local Government Finance Act 1992 (Commencement No.9 and Transitional Provision) Order 1994
items.description: This Order brings into force—

items.title: The Crown Court (Amendment) (No. 2) Rules 1994
items.description: These Rules amend the Crown Court Rules 1982 to govern procedures arising out of provisions of the Drug Trafficking Act 1994 and the Criminal Justice Act 1988, deriving from the Criminal Justice Act 1993. Rule 25A governs the procedure to be followed where a statement or other document is tendered to the Crown Court under section 11 of the 1994 Act. Rule 34 contains procedural provisions where an application is made by the defendant or the prosecutor under section 3(5)(a) of the 1994 Act or section 72A(5)(a) of the 1988 Act. Rule 35 specifies the procedure a prosecutor must follow in making an application under section 13, 14 or 15 of the 1994 Act. Rule 36 states that when a Crown Court cancels a confiscation order under section 22(2) of the 1994 Act notice of that cancellation must be given to the High Court and to the magistrates' court responsible for enforcing the order.

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts (Miscellaneous Amendments) Rules 1994
The Family Proceedings (Amendment) (No. 4) Rules 1994
items.description: This version of the The Family Proceedings (Amendment) (No. 4) Rules 1994 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Family Proceedings Courts (Children Act 1989) (Amendment (No.2) Rules 1994
items.description: This version of the The Family Proceedings Courts (Children Act 1989) (Amendment (No.2) Rules 1994 is published under the authority and superintendence of the Queen's Printer of Acts of Parliament and Controller of HMSO. It has been prepared to reflect the text as it was Made.

items.title: The Parental Responsibility Agreement (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the Parental Responsibility Agreement Regulations 1991 by prescribing a new form for the recording of an agreement between the parents of a child that the father is to have parental responsibility for the child within the meaning of section 3 of the Children Act 1989. The layout and content of the form have been improved to make it easier to understand and complete.

items.title: The Severn Bridges Tolls Order 1994
The Unfair Terms in Consumer Contracts Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 93/13/EEC on unfair terms in consumer contracts (OJ No. L95, 21.4.93, p. 29).

items.title: The East Glamorgan National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order establishes East Glamorgan National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Hastings Borough Council, Worthing Borough Council and Barnet London Borough Council) Order 1994
The Gloucester Harbour Revision Order 1994
The Telecommunication Meters (Approval Fees) (BABT) (Amendment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order amends the Telecommunication Meters (Approval Fees) (British Approvals Board for Telecommunications) Order 1992 so as to increase the fees payable for the approval and monitoring of telecommunication meters from £43.24 per man-hour to £50.68 per man-hour. It also makes minor drafting amendments.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Competition) (Legal Services) (England) Regulations 1994
items.description: These regulations, which apply to England only, make provision under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 (“the Act”) in respect of the defined activity of legal services.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Competition) (Supervision of Parking, Management of Vehicles and Security Work) (England) Regulations 1994
items.description: Regulation 2 applies the competitive tendering requirements in Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 to the defined activities of the supervision of parking; management of vehicles; and security work, as from the dates specified in respect of different classes of authority.

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Competition) (Construction and Property Services) (England) Regulations 1994
items.description: These regulations make provision under Part I of the Local Government Act 1988 (“the Act”) in respect of the defined activity of construction and property services.

items.title: The Local Government Changes for England (Direct Labour and Service Organisations) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the application of the competition provisions of the Local Government, Planning and Land Act 1980 (“the 1980 Act”) and the Local Government Act 1988 in relation to local authorities affected by structural change under the Local Government Act 1992.

items.title: The Ogwr (Ogmore Valley and Garw Valley Communities) Order 1994
The Medicines (Veterinary Drugs) (Pharmacy and Merchants' List) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1994
items.description: This Order further amends the Medicines (Veterinary Drugs) (Pharmacy and Merchants' List) Order 1992 (the “principal Order") so as to alter the fees payable on registration, retention of registration and restoration of registration of agricultural merchants and saddlers under that Order. The old and new fees are set out in the new Schedule 5 in the Schedule to this Order.”

items.title: The Morriston Hospital National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order establishes Morriston Hospital National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Council Tax (Reduction of Liability) (Scotland) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the reduction in certain circumstances of amounts payable by way of council tax and council water charge in respect of dwellings in Scotland. Where, on or after the date of these Regulations coming into force, a local assessor alters a valuation list so as to place a dwelling retrospectively in a more expensive valuation band (and that alteration is not made to reflect a material increase in the value of the dwelling since the inception of the council tax regime), liability in respect of the dwelling will in most cases be calculated as if the alteration did not have effect until the first day of the month following that in which it is made.

items.title: The General Medical Council (Constitution of Fitness to Practise Committees) (Amendment No. 2) Rules Order of Council 1994
The Broadcasting (Unlicensed Television Services) Exemption Order 1994
The Bournewood Community and Mental Health National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order establishes the Bournewood Community and Mental Health National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 7).

items.title: The Manchester Children’s Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order establishes the Manchester Children’s Hospitals National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. items.description: This Order establishes the United Leeds Teaching Hospitals National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. items.title: The Air Navigation (Dangerous Goods) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations consolidate the Air Navigation (Dangerous Goods) Regulations 1985 as amended. In addition to some minor drafting amendments the following changes are made:

items.title: The Deregulation and Contracting Out Act 1994 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1994
items.description: This Order brings into force on 3rd January 1995 provisions of the Deregulation and Contracting Out Act 1994 relating to newspaper mergers; Restrictive Trade Practices: registration of commercially sensitive information; Bars in licensed premises in England and Wales: Children’s Certificates; Employment Agencies etc.: Replacement of licensing; Unfair Dismissal: selection for redundancy; and certain provisions relating to goods and public service vehicle (“PSV”) operator licensing etc.; together with associated repeals. It brings into force on 1st April provision relating to objections to applications for PSV licences. It makes transitional provision in connection with the coming into force of sections 36(1) (Unfair dismissal: selection for redundancy) and 60 (Objections to applications for PSV licences).

items.title: The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (South Norfolk District Council) Order 1994
The Local Government Act 1988 (Defined Activities) (Exemption) (Southwark London Borough Council) Order 1994
The Criminal Justice Act 1991 (Commencement No. 4) Order 1994
The Criminal Justice and Public Order Act 1994 (Commencement No. 2) Order 1994
The Criminal Justice Act 1991 (Suspension of Prisoner Custody Officer Certificate) (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: Paragraph 3 of Schedule 10 to the Criminal Justice Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”) provides that if it appears to the appropriate prisoner escort monitor, controller of a contracted out prison or governor of a directly managed prison, that a prisoner custody officer is not a fit and proper person to perform, respectively, escort functions, custodial duties or contracted out functions, he may refer the matter to the Secretary of State who may revoke that officer’s certificate and may, in prescribed circumstances, suspend the officer’s certificate pending the Secretary of State’s decision.

items.title: The Young Offender Institution (Amendment) Rules 1994
items.description: These Rules amend the Young Offender Institution Rules 1988 (“the 1988 Rules”), as amended.

items.title: The Prison (Amendment) Rules 1994
items.description: These Rules amend the Prison Rules 1964 (“the 1964 Rules”), as amended.

items.title: The Social Security (Claims and Payments) Amendment (No. 4) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the Social Security (Claims and Payments) Regulations 1987 in the following respects—

items.title: The Wolverhampton Health Care National Health Service Trust (Establishment) Order 1994
items.description: This Order establishes the Wolverhampton Health Care National Health Service Trust, an NHS trust provided for in section 5 of the National Health Service and Community Care Act 1990. It also provides for the functions of the trust both before (article 6) and after (article 3) its operational date (the date on which it assumes all its functions). It specifies the operational date and the accounting date of the trust (article 5) and makes provision for assistance to the trust by health authorities before its operational date (article 7). It specifies the value of assets in excess of which the Secretary of State is to consider the disposal of the asset (article 8).

items.title: The Firearms (Scotland) Amendment Rules 1994
items.description: These Rules amend the form of firearm certificate prescribed by the Firearms (Scotland) Rules 1989 by substituting five years for three years in the references to the period for which the certificate is granted or renewed, as the case may be. This is a consequence of the period laid down in section 26(3) of the Firearms Act 1968 being so extended by the Firearms (Period of Certificate) Order 1994 (S.I. 1994/2614).

items.title: The Valuation for Rating (Plant and Machinery) (Scotland) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations specify the classes of plant and machinery which, as from 1st April 1995, will be rateable in Scotland for the purposes of non-domestic rating. The Regulations reflect the recommendations contained in the Report of the Expert Advisory Committee chaired by Mr Derek Wood QC (Cm 2170). The Committee reviewed the rating of plant and machinery with a view to updating and harmonising it throughout the United Kingdom.

items.title: The Freeman Group of Hospitals National Health Service Trust (Transfer of Trust Property) Order 1994
items.description: This Order transfers to the Freeman Group of Hospitals National Health Service Trust on 9th February 1994 trust property formerly held by the Newcastle Health Authority.  A copy of the schedule of trust property to which the Order refers can be obtained at the Department of Health, National Health Service Trust Unit, Quarry House, Quarry Hill, Leeds LS2 7UE.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Unoccupied Property) (Scotland) Regulations 1994
items.description: Sections 24 and 24A of the Local Government (Scotland) Act 1966 (as substituted and inserted respectively by sections 154 and 155 of the Local Government etc. (Scotland) Act 1994) provide as to the rating of unoccupied property and of property partially unoccupied for a short time.Where property is completely unoccupied, no rates are payable except in the case of property falling within a class prescribed by regulations (in which case the non-domestic rate is payable at half the normal level). Where property is partially unoccupied for a short time and the relevant rating authority requests the assessor to apportion the rateable value between the occupied and unoccupied parts, rates are only payable in respect of the value apportioned to the occupied part except in the case of property falling within a class prescribed by regulations (in which case rates are also levied on half the rateable value apportioned to the unoccupied part).

items.title: The Child Abduction and Custody (Parties to Conventions) (Amendment) (No. 6) Order 1994
The Consular Fees (Amendment) Order 1994
The European Convention on Extradition (Amendment) Order 1994
The Food and Environment Protection Act 1985 (Isle of Man) (Revocation) Order 1994
The Ministerial and other Salaries Order 1994
items.description: This Order increases salaries payable under the Ministerial and other Salaries Act 1975 to Ministers, to salaried Members of the Opposition, to the Speaker of the House of Commons and to other paid office holders. The amounts specified in this Order (other than Part II of Schedule 1) are the maximum salaries payable. The actual salaries in payment may therefore be below these levels.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Estonia) Order 1994
The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Isle of Man) Order 1994
The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Guernsey) Order 1994
The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Jersey) Order 1994
The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Kazakhstan) Order 1994
items.description: The Convention with Kazakhstan is set out in the Schedule to the Order.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Mexico) Order 1994
items.description: The Convention with Mexico is set out in the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Russian Federation) Order 1994
The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Estates of Deceased Persons and Inheritances) (Switzerland) Order 1994
items.description: The Convention with Switzerland is set out in Part I of the Schedule to this Order.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Switzerland) Order 1994
items.description: The Protocol scheduled to this Order makes certain alterations to the Convention set out in the Schedule to the Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Switzerland) Order 1978 as amended by the arrangements set out in the schedule to the Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Switzerland) Order 1982.

items.title: The Double Taxation Relief (Taxes on Income) (Vietnam) Order 1994
The Maximum Number of Judges Order 1994
items.description: This Order amends the Administration of Justice Act 1968 by increasing the maximum number of Lords of Appeal in Ordinary from 11 to 12. The Order also amends the Supreme Court Act 1981 by increasing the maximum number of ordinary judges of the Court of Appeal from 29 to 32.

items.title: The European Convention on Cinematographic Co-production (Amendment) (No. 2) Order 1994
The Designs (Convention Countries) Order 1994
items.description: This Order, made pursuant to section 13(1) of the Registered Designs Act 1949, declares the countries specified in Schedule 1 to be convention countries for the purposes of all the provisions of that Act. The majority of countries specified in this Order have acceded to the International Convention for the Protection of Industrial Property (“the Paris Convention”) (as revised at Stockholm in 1967) (Cmnd. 4431). Most of them have been declared to be convention countries pursuant to 67 separate orders, the earliest of which was made in 1901 and the last in 1993. All these orders, in so far as they have effect in relation to designs, are made or treated as having been made under section 13(1) of the Registered Designs Act 1949. This Order revokes the orders making the earlier declarations in respect of designs and brings together all declarations in respect of such countries for the purposes of all the provisions of the Registered Designs Act 1949 into one instrument.

items.title: The Patents (Convention Countries) Order 1994
items.description: This Order, pursuant to section 90(1) of the Patents Act 1977, declares the countries specified in Schedue 1 to be convention countries for the purposes of section 5 of the Patents Act 1977. The majority of countries specified in this Order have acceded to the International Convention for the Protection of Industrial Property (“the Paris Convention”) (as revised at Stockholm in 1967) (Cmnd. 4431). Most of them have been declared to be convention countries pursuant to 67 separate orders, the earliest of which was made in 1901 and the last in 1993. All these orders, so far as they have effect in relation to patents, are made or treated as having been made under section 90(1) of the Patents Act 1977 for the purposes of section 5 of that Act. This Order revokes the orders making the earlier declarations in respect of patents and brings together all declarations in respect of such countries for the purposes of section 5 of the Patents Act 1977 into one instrument.

items.title: The Local Government Superannuation (Local Government Reorganisation in England) Regulations 1994
items.description: These regulations make provision in connection with the Local Government Superannuation Regulations 1986 (“the principal Regulations”) and the effects on them of local government reorganisation in England under the Local Government Act 1992.

items.title: The Films Co-Production Agreements (Amendment) Order 1994
The Local Government Changes for England (Finance, Miscellaneous Provisions) Regulations 1994
items.description: Part II of the Local Government Act 1992 makes provision for local government changes in England. Where recommendations for change are made by the Local Government Commission, the Secretary of State may make an order giving effect to those recommendations (“a reorganisation order”). The Local Government Changes for England (Finance) Regulations 1994 make transitional financial provisions in relation to reorganisation orders.

items.title: The Finance Act 1993, section 165, (Appointed Day) Order 1994
The Finance Act 1994, Chapter II of Part IV, (Appointed Day) Order 1994
The Exchange Gains and Losses (Transitional Provisions) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 23rd March 1995, apply to assets, currency contracts and liabilities held or owed by a company immediately before the provisions relating to exchange gains and losses contained in Chapter II of the Finance Act 1993 first apply as respects that company.

items.title: The Exchange Gains and Losses (Alternative Method of Calculation of Gain or Loss) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 23rd March 1995, make provision for an alternative method of calculation to be used in certain circumstances for finding the amount to be treated as an exchange gain or loss under the provisions in Chapter II of Part II of the Finance Act 1993.

items.title: The Exchange Gains and Losses (Deferral of Gains and Losses) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 23rd March 1995, supplement the provisions in sections 139 to 143 of the Finance Act 1993 which allow companies to defer, from one accrual period to the next, recognition of certain unrealised exchange gains on long-term capital borrowings and advances.

items.title: The Exchange Gains and Losses (Excess Gains and Losses) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 23rd March 1995, make provision for amounts to be treated as exchange gains and losses in certain circumstances.

items.title: The Education (School Financial Statements) (Prescribed Particulars etc.) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace for a financial year beginning on or after 1st April 1994 the Education (School Financial Statements) (Prescribed Particulars etc.) Regulations 1993. Those Regulations (except insofar as they impose requirements in respect of section 42(4)(f) to (i) of the Education Reform Act 1988, being provisions repealed by section 275 of the Education Act 1993) continue to apply for the financial year beginning 1st April 1993 and the Education (School Financial Statements) (Prescribed Particulars etc.) Regulations 1990 continue to apply for earlier financial years.

items.title: The Local Currency Elections Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 23rd March 1995, make provision for the basic profits or losses of a trade or part trade to be computed and expressed for corporation tax purposes in a currency other than sterling where a company so elects and where certain conditions are satisfied.

items.title: The Exchange Gains and Losses (Insurance Companies) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations, which come into force on 23rd March 1995, modify the provisions relating to exchange gains and losses contained in Chapter II of Part II of the Finance Act 1993 in their application to assets and liabilities held or owed by insurance companies.

items.title: The Exchange Gains and Losses (Debts of Varying Amounts) Regulations 1994
The Currency Contracts and Options (Amendment of Enactments) Order 1994
The Environmental Protection Act 1990 (Commencement No. 15) (Amendment No. 2) Order 1994
The Merchant Shipping (Reporting Requirements for Ships Carrying Dangerous or Polluting Goods) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with amendments the Merchant Shipping (Reporting of Pollution Incidents) Regulations 1987.

items.title: The Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations re-enact, with minor modifications, the Control of Substances Hazardous to Health Regulations 1988 (S. I.1988/1657) (“the 1988 Regulations”) as amended. The 1988 Regulations imposed duties on employers to protect employees and other persons who may be exposed to substances hazardous to health and also imposed certain duties on employees concerning their own protection from such exposure (regulations 1 to 17 and Schedules 1 to 8). The Regulations also prohibit the import into the United Kingdom of certain substances and articles from outside the European Economic Area referred to in the Agreement defined in regulation 2(1) thereof.

items.title: The Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging for Supply) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations revoke and re-enact with amendments the Chemicals (Hazard Information and Packaging) Regulations 1993 (S.I. 1993/1746) (“the 1993 Regulations”). The 1993 Regulations were substantially amended by the Carriage of Dangerous Goods by Road and Rail (Classification, Packaging and Labelling) Regulations 1994 (S.I. 1994/669) which revoked those parts of the 1993 Regulations which regulated the classification, packaging and labelling for carriage by road of substances dangerous for carriage.

items.title: The Price Indications (Resale of Tickets) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations regulate the manner in which a person who is or may be prepared to resell tickets by way of business gives consumers an indication as to the price at which the ticket is, or would be, available (regulation 3).

items.title: The Welfare of Animals during Transport Order 1994
items.description: This Order revokes and re-enacts with modifications the Welfare of Animals during Transport Order 1992. Taken with other welfare legislation, it implements Council Directive 91/628/EEC on the protection of animals during transport (OJ No. L340, 11.12.91, p.17). The other legislation referred to is as follows—

items.title: The Magistrates' Courts Fees (Amendment) Order 1994
The Education (Inter-authority Recoupment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 51 of the Education (No. 2) Act 1986 as amended by section 279 of the Education Act 1993. They introduce a number of changes to the recoupment system.

items.title: The Local Authorities (Recognised Bodies for Heritable Securities Indemnities) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1994
The National Health Service Trusts (Appointment of Trustees) (Scotland) (No.2) Order 1994
items.description: This Order appoints the chairman, non-executive directors and executive directors and their successors in office of each of the National Health Service Trusts specified in the Schedule to the Order as trustees for the purposes of holding any property which is to be held in trust on behalf of that Trust.

items.title: The Local Government (Transitional Election Arrangements) (Scotland) Order 1994
items.description: In connection with local government reorganisation in Scotland, elections are to be held on 6th April 1995 to new mainland councils. This Order makes provision in respect of those elections and in respect of elections for casual vacancies occurring in the membership of those new councils between 6th April 1995 and 1st April 1996. The Order applies local election law with necessary modifications and will cease to have effect after 31st March 1996.

items.title: The Valuation for Rating (Decapitalisation Rate) (Scotland) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the decapitalisation rate to be applied when valuing lands and heritages in Scotland in accordance with the contractor’s principle for the purposes of any valuation roll which comes into force on or after 1st April 1995 (and restrict the Valuation for Rating (Decapitalisation Rate) (Scotland) Regulations 1990 so that they will not apply in respect of valuations for the purposes of any such roll).

items.title: The Registration of Births, Deaths and Marriages (Fees) Order 1994 (revoked)
items.description: This Order specifies the fees payable under the Acts relating to the registration of births, deaths and marriages and associated matters.

items.title: The Criminal Justice and Public Order Act 1994 (Commencement No. 3) Order 1994
The Electricity (Non-Fossil Fuel Sources) (England and Wales) Order 1994
items.description: This Order imposes on public electricity suppliers in England and Wales obligations to make arrangements to secure the availability during specified periods of specified aggregate amounts of generating capacity from non-fossil fuel generating stations of particular descriptions. The arrangements must be made, and evidence of their making must be produced to the Director General of Electricity Supply, before 20th January 1995.

items.title: The Inshore Fishing (Prohibition of Fishing and Fishing Methods) (Scotland) Amendment Order 1994
items.description: This Order amends the Inshore Fishing (Prohibition of Fishing and Fishing Methods) (Scotland) Order 1989 by—

items.title: The Electrical Equipment (Safety) Regulations 1994 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations consolidate with amendments the Low Voltage Electrical Equipment (Safety) Regulations 1989 (the “1989 Regulations”). They implement the requirements of Council Directive No. 73/23/EEC (OJ No. L77, 26.3.1973, p.29) on the harmonisation of the laws of member States relating to electrical equipment designed for use within certain voltage limits, as amended by Council Directive No. 93/68/EEC (OJ No. L220, 30.8.1993, p.1) (the “CE marking” Directive).

items.title: The Police and Magistrates' Courts Act 1994 (Commencement No. 5 and Transitional Provisions) Order 1994
items.description: This Order brings into force for all purposes most of those provisions in Part I of the Police and Magistrates' Courts Act 1994 (which concerns the arrangements for policing in England and Wales) which are not currently in force or, as the case may be, which are in force for certain purposes only. The provisions which are not included in this Order and not in force relate to police discipline (including the police appeals tribunals).

items.title: The Highways (Inquiries Procedure) Rules 1994
items.description: These Rules regulate the procedures to be followed in England and Wales in respect of public local inquiries which are held in connection with schemes and orders proposed to be made by the Secretary of State or made by local highway authorities under Part II of the Highways Act 1980.

items.title: The Compulsory Purchase by Ministers (Inquiries Procedure) Rules 1994
items.description: They replace the Compulsory Purchase by Ministers (Inquiries Procedure) Rules 1967 (S. I.1967/720), which are revoked (subject to the transitional provisions contained in rule 21(2)) and generally contain similar procedures to those laid down in the Highways (Inquiries Procedure) Rules 1994 (S. I.1994/3263).

items.title: The Court of Session etc. Fees Amendment Order 1994
The High Court of Justiciary Fees Amendment Order 1994
Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) (No.2) 1994
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 1994 (S.I. 1994/392) by increasing the fees payable to Sheriff Officers by about 3%.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) (No.2) 1994
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 1994 (S.I. 1994/391) by increasing the fees payable to Messengers-at-Arms by about 3%.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the Town and Country Planning (Fees for Applications and Deemed Applications) (Scotland) Regulations 1990 (“the principal Regulations”), which make provision for the payment of fees to planning authorities in respect of—

items.title: The Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) (Amendment) (No.3) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Road Vehicles (Construction and Use) Regulations 1986 by—

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Registration of Local Services) (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles (Registration of Local Services) Regulations 1986 so as to limit the circumstances in which a local bus service registered under the Transport Act 1985 can be varied by the running of duplicate buses, without the registration being varied.

items.title: The Public Service Vehicles (Traffic Regulation Conditions) (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Service Vehicles (Traffic Regulation Conditions) Regulations 1986 by prescribing an additional purpose for which traffic commissioners may, pursuant to section 7 of the Transport Act 1985, determine traffic regulation conditions applying to local bus services registered under section 6 of that Act.

items.title: The Sole and Nephrops (Prohibition of Fishing) Order 1994
items.description: This Order prohibits, subject to an exception, fishing for sole(Solea solea)and nephrops(Nephrops norvegicus)during the period beginning with the date of coming into force of the Order and ending immediately before 1st January 1995—

items.title: The Electricity (Non-Fossil Fuel Sources) (Scotland) Order 1994
items.description: This Order imposes on public electricity suppliers in Scotland obligations to make arrangements to secure the availability during specified periods of specified aggregate amounts of generating capacity from non-fossil fuel generating stations of particular descriptions. The arrangements must be made, and evidence of their making must be produced to the Director General of Electricity Supply, before 20th January 1995.

items.title: The Sex Discrimination Act 1975 (Application to Armed Forces etc) Regulations 1994 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations are made under section 2(2) of the European Communities Act 1972 and amend the Sex Discrimination Act 1975 (“the 1975 Act”) so that the 1975 Act accords with the obligations arising under Council Directive 76/207/EEC (OJ No. L39, 14.2.76, p40) in relation to the armed forces of the Crown.

items.title: The National Savings Stock Register (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Savings Stock Register Regulations 1976. Regulation 12 of those Regulations provides for commission to be payable to the Director of Savings when stock on the National Savings Stock Register is purchased or sold through him. The rates of commission are set out in Schedule 1 to the Regulations. These Regulations alter the rates of commission due.

items.title: The Overseas Life Assurance Fund (Amendment) Order 1994
The Non-Domestic Rating (Chargeable Amounts) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the period of five years beginning on 1 April 1995, in relation to non-domestic rates under Part III of the Local Government Finance Act 1988 (“the 1988 Act”), as to the chargeable amount for which a ratepayer is liable in certain circumstances. They also make provisions as to the giving of certificates by valuation officers.

items.title: The Goods Vehicles (Plating and Testing) (Amendment) Regulations 1994
items.description: These Regulations further amend the Goods Vehicles (Plating and Testing) Regulations 1988 and are made in consequence of S.I. 1994/329. The 1988 Regulations make provisions for vehicles to be examined and issued with plating certificates.

items.title: The Docks and Harbours (Rateable Values) (Amendment) Order 1994
The British Waterways Board and Telecommunications Industry (Rateable Values) Revocation Order 1994
The Electricity Supply Industry (Rateable Values) Order 1994
items.description: Under paragraph 3(1) of Schedule 6 to the Local Government Finance Act 1988, the Secretary of State may by order provide that a hereditament of a description prescribed in the order is not to be valued for non-domestic rating on the basis set out in paragraphs 2 to 2B of that Schedule (that is to say, by reference to the rent a hypothetical tenant would pay for the hereditament on an annual basis), but on the basis of prescribed rules.

items.description: These Regulations amend the Road Vehicles (Registration and Licensing) Regulations (Northern Ireland) Regulations 1973. Regulation 7(1) (duplicate licences) of the 1973 Regulations is amended to make it clear that it applies to both vehicle and trade licences and the fee payable for the issue of a duplicate licence is increased from £5.50 to £7. The other amendments are to regulation 32 (trade plates). A motorcycle dealer is only entitled to one additional trade plate. If a trade plate is lost, stolen, damaged or destroyed the whole set must be replaced. The fees for the issue of a duplicate set of trade plates are fixed at £13.50 for a set of two trade plates and £18 for a set of three. A fee of £2 is prescribed for the issue of a replacement holder for the display of a trade licence.

The Industrial and Provident Societies (Increase in Shareholding Limit) Order 1994
items.description: This Order increases from £10,000 to £20,000 the limit on the interest which any one member may hold in the shares of a society registered under the Industrial and Provident Societies Act 1965. It further empowers the committee of a society, subject to certain conditions, to give effect to the increase by resolution recorded in writing. If the rules happen to be amended subsequently and do not make reference to the increased limit resolved on by the committee of the society the shareholding limit reverts to that in the rules as previously registered. Where an amendment makes provision as to the limit, on registration of the amendment the limit is the limit so provided.
